name: NeMo Data Designer
tagline: Build production-grade synthetic datasets with samplers, LLMs, and validation
seoTitle: NeMo Data Designer - Synthetic Data Generation
metaDescription: NeMo Data Designer is an NVIDIA Python library for generating synthetic datasets with statistical samplers, LLM columns, validators, and LLM-as-a-judge scoring.
overview: NeMo Data Designer is an open-source Python library from NVIDIA for generating synthetic datasets, either from scratch or from your own seed data. Instead of one-off LLM prompting, you describe a dataset as a set of columns, where each column is filled by a statistical sampler, an LLM, or an existing dataset.

It is aimed at developers and ML teams who need realistic training or test data with control over field distributions and the relationships between fields. You can validate generated rows with Python, SQL, and custom local or remote validators, and score outputs using an LLM as a judge.

As a synthetic-data and labeling tool, it sits between simple prompt scripts and full data platforms: a configuration-driven framework where you build a column schema, preview a sample, then run full generation. It works with NVIDIA Build, OpenAI, and OpenRouter model providers out of the box.
features: Generate data from statistical samplers, LLM columns, or existing seed datasets
Dependency-aware generation that controls relationships between fields
Built-in Python, SQL, and custom local or remote validators for quality checks
LLM-as-a-judge scoring to assess output quality
Preview mode to inspect sample records before full-scale generation
Async engine (default) that overlaps independent columns and adapts concurrency per provider and model

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package, set a provider API key, then define columns and preview a sample before generating at scale.

steps.heading: Set your API key
steps.body: Set the environment variable for one of the default providers (NVIDIA Build, OpenAI, or OpenRouter).

steps.heading: Define columns and preview
steps.body: Build a config with a sampler column and an LLM text column, then preview a sample record.
steps.code:
import data_designer.config as dd
from data_designer.interface import DataDesigner

data_designer = DataDesigner()
config_builder = dd.DataDesignerConfigBuilder()

config_builder.add_column(
    dd.SamplerColumnConfig(
        name="product_category",
        sampler_type=dd.SamplerType.CATEGORY,
        params=dd.CategorySamplerParams(
            values=["Electronics", "Clothing", "Home & Kitchen", "Books"],
        ),
    )
)

config_builder.add_column(
    dd.LLMTextColumnConfig(
        name="review",
        model_alias="nvidia-text",
        prompt="Write a brief product review for a {{ product_category }} item you recently purchased.",
    )
)

preview = data_designer.preview(config_builder=config_builder)
preview.display_sample_record()

steps.heading: Configure providers and models via CLI
steps.body: Use the CLI to set up model providers and view current settings.
steps.code:
data-designer config providers
data-designer config models
data-designer config list

useCases: Generate synthetic training data with controlled statistical distributions and field correlations
Create realistic test datasets from a small seed dataset for ML pipelines
Produce validated, scored sample data (for example product reviews tied to categories) for prototyping
Augment existing data with dependency-aware fields while checking quality with Python or SQL validators
